item.title: Setting user permissions in KVM
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
In a preôÙvious artiôÙcle I menôÙtioôÙneôÙd how to insôÙtaôÙll a liôÙbraôÙry in FeôÙdoôÙra, in orôÙder to make KVM virôÙtuaôÙliôÙzaôÙtion eaôÙsieôÙr, maôÙnaôÙging the NAT neôÙtwoôÙrk conôÙfiôÙguôÙraôÙtion beôÙtween the guest virôÙtuaôÙl ôÙmaôÙchiôÙne and the hosôÙt, by means of libôÙvirt.

BeôÙsiôÙdes thaôÙt, whiôÙle using KVM loôÙcaôÙlly for deôÙveôÙloôÙpôÙmenôÙt, I use viôÙrôÙôÙt-ôÙôÙmaôÙôÙnaôÙôÙger, a helôÙpful appliôÙcaôÙtion that maôÙnaôÙges the diôÙffeôÙrent virôÙtuaôÙl ôÙmaôÙchiôÙnes. This appliôÙcaôÙtioôÙn, as weôÙll as the rest of the coôÙmôÙmanôÙds that inôÙteôÙracôÙt wiôÙth libôÙvirt (virsh for examôÙple), reôÙquiôÙre suôÙper user priôÙviôÙleôÙges, so it wiôÙll prompt for the suôÙdo paôÙssword eveôÙry tiôÙme.

This can be avoiôÙded by inôÙcluôÙding the user inôÙto the foôÙlloôÙwing groupôÙs: kvm, and libôÙvirt.

TheôÙreôÙfoôÙre, just by runôÙning the foôÙlloôÙwing coôÙmôÙmand we can skip the paôÙssworôÙd ôÙprompt eveôÙry tiôÙme.

sudo usermod -a -G kvm,libvirt mariano

This is an opôÙtion I would use onôÙly for loôÙcal deôÙveôÙloôÙpôÙment on my maôÙchiôÙne. ôÙProôÙducôÙtiôÙve enôÙviôÙronôÙmenôÙts must haôÙve an strict perôÙmisôÙsions maôÙnaôÙgeôÙmenôÙt.

item.title: Libvirt networking libraries
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
FeôÙdoôÙra 21 woôÙrksôÙtaôÙtion seeôÙms to coôÙme wiôÙth a lot of virôÙtuaôÙliôÙzaôÙtion feaôÙtuôÙres and most of the libôÙvirt liôÙbraôÙries insôÙtaôÙlleôÙd. I onôÙly had to add the KVM viôÙrôÙôÙtuaôÙôÙl-ôÙôÙmaôÙôÙnaôÙôÙger whiôÙch is ôÙthe KVM appliôÙcaôÙtion I am moôÙre faôÙmiôÙliar wiôÙth. HoôÙweôÙveôÙr, the new verôÙsion of the libôÙvirôÙt* liôÙbraôÙrieôÙs haôÙve neôÙtwoôÙrking feaôÙtuôÙres that are great for the daôÙta cenôÙtre enôÙviôÙronôÙmenôÙt, but maôÙyôÙbe not the best opôÙtioôÙn ôÙfor a parôÙtiôÙcuôÙlar woôÙrksôÙtaôÙtioôÙn, so I added the foôÙlloôÙwing paôÙckaôÙges in orôÙder to set up an eaôÙsieôÙr ôÙneôÙtwoôÙrk conôÙfiôÙguôÙraôÙtion for my loôÙcal virôÙtual maôÙchiôÙnes.

sudo dnf install virt-install libvirt-daemon-config-network

In orôÙder to reôÙflect the chanôÙges and start using the new feaôÙtuôÙres, we need to resôÙtart its serôÙviôÙce:

systemctl restart libvirtd.service

After thaôÙt, when creaôÙting a new virôÙtual maôÙchiôÙne, the NAT opôÙtion is enaôÙbleôÙd, and the virôÙtuaôÙl ôÙmaôÙnaôÙger wiôÙth handôÙle the NAT or briôÙdging conôÙfiôÙguôÙraôÙtion auôÙtoôÙmaôÙtiôÙcaôÙllôÙy, whiôÙch alloôÙws me to deôÙploy new ôÙmaôÙchiôÙnes fasôÙteôÙr.
